title: Secret Leak Review
description: Inspect changed source, logs, errors, telemetry, and artifacts for raw or recoverable secrets.
body: Inputs: diff, generated files, logs, config examples, and artifact paths. Use when secrets, credentials, tokens, or signing material may be present. Do not reveal, copy, or transform real secret values. Procedure: search for high-entropy or named secret patterns, inspect output sinks, verify redaction before emission, and mark any exposed real value as an incident requiring human rotation. Output: safe findings with values omitted, affected sinks, and validation gaps. Validation signals include redacted snapshots and artifact checks. Use when Security Risk Controls work touches trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
